task: When a user searches for a building/community (e.g., "Marina Gate 1") that doesn't exist in our database, dynamically scrape information from external sources (using BrightData API) and create an informative community page. Store the scraped data in MongoDB (binayah_web_new_dev, new collection). The page design should match the existing projects page but be adapted for informational purposes (clearly not a property we're selling). Scraping is triggered on search—if the community isn't found locally, fetch data, display the page, and cache it.

stages.spec:
## Stage 1 — Community Info Pages (full feature, single stage)

### Why this will be good
A real-estate search that returns nothing today is a dead-end. With this feature, the same query becomes the start of an informational journey: the user lands on a community page that looks and feels like our project pages, learns about Marina Gate 1, and is gently invited to explore similar properties we DO sell. The delight bar: the page should feel curated (real hero image scraped from the source, clean section cards, prominent location), not like a Wikipedia stub. Production-ready means: graceful 'no info' when scraping fails, hidden sections when fields are absent (never an empty 'Amenities' card), Community Information badge that makes the read-only nature obvious, and zero listing-affordances (no Favorite, no Subscribe, no Buy/Rent CTAs). Benchmark: Bayut and Property Finder community guides — image-led, info-dense, clearly distinct from listing pages.

### Scope — build all of this in one PR

#### A. New API route: `/api/community-info` (Next.js, server-side)
- File: `src/app/api/community-info/route.ts`
- Accepts `GET ?q=<search term>`.
- Slug normalization: lowercase, kebab-case (`Marina Gate 1` → `marina-gate-1`). Use the same util the rest of the app uses if one exists; otherwise add a small helper in `src/lib/`.
- Lookup order:
  1. Connect to MongoDB `binayah_web_new_dev` (use the existing connection helper — check `src/lib/mongo*` or `src/lib/db*`).
  2. Query `community_info_pages` collection for `{ slug }`. If found, return `{ exists: true, data }` immediately (cache hit, no scrape).
  3. On miss, invoke the scraper (see B). If scraper returns usable data, insert into `community_info_pages` and return `{ exists: true, data }`.
  4. If scraper returns nothing usable from all 3 sources, return `{ exists: false }`. Do NOT insert an empty document — next search retries.
- Error handling: any thrown error returns `{ exists: false }` with HTTP 200. Never 500. Log the error server-side for debugging.
- Mongo schema (collection `community_info_pages`):
  ```ts
  {
    _id: ObjectId,
    slug: string,            // unique index
    name: string,
    location?: string,
    description?: string,
    developerName?: string,
    heroImage?: string,
    amenities?: string[],
    priceRange?: { min: number, max: number, currency: string },
    sources: string[],       // which of the 3 URLs contributed
    scrapedAt: Date
  }
  ```
  Create a unique index on `slug` (idempotent — `createIndex` is safe to call repeatedly).

#### B. Scraper service (server-side only)
- File: `src/lib/communityScraper.ts` (or co-located in the route file if small enough — DEV decides).
- BrightData Web Unlocker — credentials live in `/Users/zoop/.env.shared`. Read the keys, add to `.env.local`, and push to Vercel via the Vercel CLI/API as part of the deploy. Document the env var names in a comment at the top of the scraper.
- Three target URLs, hit in order:
  1. `https://www.keyone.com/dubai-communities`
  2. `https://www.tanamiproperties.com/Communities`
  3. `https://stageproperties.com/areas-and-communities`
- Strategy: for each source, fetch the directory page (or a search-filtered version if the site supports it), then parse for entries matching the user's query. Use `cheerio` for HTML parsing (already in deps; if not, add it).
- Per-source timeout: 10s. On timeout or non-2xx, skip and try next source. Track which sources succeeded in `sources` array.
- Merge strategy: first source with a name match wins for the primary fields (name, description, heroImage). Subsequent sources fill any still-empty fields (amenities, location, developerName, priceRange).
- If after all 3 sources we have no `name` match (or only a name with no other fields), return `null` so the API returns `{ exists: false }`.

#### C. Search-modal integration (frontend)
- The search bar lives in `HeroSection` and `Navbar.tsx`. Find the existing search-result handler (it likely calls `/api/search` or the Fastify backend) and locate the zero-results branch.
- When the search returns zero project/listing results AND the query is non-trivial (≥ 3 chars), call `/api/community-info?q=<term>`.
- If the response is `{ exists: true, data }`, render an additional row in the search dropdown/modal: a clickable card with the community name + 'Community information' subtitle, linking to `/communities/[slug]`. Use the slug returned from the API (don't recompute client-side — server is the source of truth).
- If the response is `{ exists: false }`, show the existing 'No results' message — DEV may optionally append a soft 'We couldn't find community info either' line, but don't make it loud. Don't block the existing UI.
- Loading: while the community-info call is in flight, the existing 'no results' state is fine — don't add a second spinner. The community card just appears when ready.
- Don't refactor the search component beyond what's needed to add this branch.

#### D. `/communities/[slug]` page — conditional render
- File: `src/app/[locale]/communities/[slug]/page.tsx` (already exists).
- New logic: if the slug matches a `community_info_pages` document, render the new community-info layout. Otherwise fall back to the existing community/project rendering.
- The community-info layout MUST visually match our existing project detail page in structure and feel — hero image, name+location header, section cards for description / amenities / price range / developer. Reuse existing project-page components/styles where reasonable so the two pages feel like siblings, not strangers.
- Required visible elements:
  - Prominent 'Community Information' badge near the top (so the read-only nature is unmistakable).
  - All available fields rendered. Fields that are absent: hide the section entirely. Never show 'Amenities: —' or empty cards.
  - Bottom CTA banner: copy 'Interested in similar properties?' linking to the main search or inquiry form (pick whichever fits the existing CTA pattern best).
- Forbidden on this page:
  - No Favorite/heart button.
  - No Subscribe/notify button.
  - No Buy/Rent/Enquire buttons.
  - No unit tabs, floor-plan tabs, or price-by-bedroom tables.
- Apply `.claude/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md` if it exists. Mobile must work at 390px. Loading skeleton while the page hydrates is fine; an explicit error state if the slug document was deleted between dispatch and render is fine too.

#### E. Env var propagation
- BrightData credentials must end up in three places per CLAUDE.md rule #7:
  1. `/Users/zoop/.env.shared` (already there — confirm).
  2. `/Users/zoop/binayah-properties/.env.local` (add).
  3. Vercel project env vars (push via CLI). DEV does this as part of the commit-and-deploy step.

### Acceptance criteria
1. Searching 'Marina Gate 1' (or any non-existent project) triggers `/api/community-info`. If scraper succeeds, the search modal shows a community-info card linking to `/communities/marina-gate-1`.
2. The community page renders with a 'Community Information' badge, hero image, available fields only, and the 'Interested in similar properties?' CTA. No favorite/subscribe/enquire buttons.
3. Searching the same term again hits the cache (verify by checking server logs — only one BrightData call in total). The Mongo document exists in `community_info_pages` and has not been re-written.
4. If scraping fails for all 3 sources, the API returns `{ exists: false }`, the search modal does NOT show a community card, no 500 errors anywhere, no broken page.
5. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. No new ESLint errors introduced.
6. Mobile (390px) renders cleanly. Existing project pages still render normally.

### Files DEV will likely touch
- New: `src/app/api/community-info/route.ts`
- New: `src/lib/communityScraper.ts` (or inline in the route)
- Edit: `src/app/[locale]/communities/[slug]/page.tsx` — conditional branch for community-info docs
- Edit: search component(s) — likely `src/components/HeroSection.tsx` and the search piece inside `src/components/Navbar.tsx` (or whatever shared SearchModal/SearchBar they both use — DEV: check first; if there's a shared component, edit ONE file, not two).
- New: small slug helper in `src/lib/` if one doesn't already exist.
- `.env.local` — add BrightData credentials.

### What DEV decides (not specified here)
- Exact Tailwind classes, spacing, badge color, hero image aspect ratio.
- Whether to extract a `<CommunityInfoPage>` component or inline the JSX.
- Cheerio selectors for each scraper source (DEV must inspect the actual HTML).
- Whether the search-modal community card is a separate visual style or matches the existing result-row style — pick whichever reads cleaner in context.
- Whether to use `React.cache` or a simple in-memory map for request-level dedupe in the route handler — both are fine, neither is required.

### Demo path
1. Open the site. Click the search bar in the navbar.
2. Type 'Marina Gate 1' (or any community name not in our project DB).
3. See the community-info card appear in the dropdown after a moment.
4. Click it → land on `/communities/marina-gate-1`.
5. See the 'Community Information' badge, hero image, description, location, amenities (whichever scraped), and the 'Interested in similar properties?' CTA at the bottom.
6. No Favorite/Subscribe/Enquire buttons anywhere on the page.
7. Search the same term again → community card appears instantly (cache hit).

plannerOutput:
## Community Info Pages -- Final Brief

### Feature summary
When a user searches for a building/community (e.g. 'Marina Gate 1') that returns zero results from the existing search, we intercept the miss and trigger a BrightData scrape of three real-estate community directory pages. The scraped result is stored permanently in a new MongoDB collection and displayed as an informational community page. This is read-only, not a listing, and not a project we sell.

### Search flow integration
- Hook into the existing search modal/bar (HeroSection + wherever the search bar in the Navbar dispatches queries).
- The search already hits /api/search (Fastify). When that returns zero results, the client (or a Next.js API wrapper) checks the new /api/community-info Next.js route with the search term.
- If a match is found (cached or freshly scraped), the search modal surfaces a prompt: 'No listings found -- view community info for [X]?' with a link to /communities/[slug].
- The communities route already exists in the router per PROJECT_CONTEXT (src/app/[locale]/communities/). Use it.
- Do NOT add a new search input or new URL path.

### Scraping sources (BrightData Web Unlocker)
Three target URLs, scraped in order (first successful parse wins; others used for supplemental fields):
1. https://www.keyone.com/dubai-communities
2. https://www.tanamiproperties.com/Communities
3. https://stageproperties.com/areas-and-communities

Scraping is server-side only (Next.js API route on Vercel). Raw HTML is parsed server-side; only structured data is stored. BrightData credentials live in .env.shared -- push to Vercel after reading.

### Data schema -- community_info_pages collection (binayah_web_new_dev)
Fields are whatever the scrapers can actually extract; not all are guaranteed. Store what exists.

```ts
{
  _id: ObjectId,
  slug: string,            // kebab-case of community name, used as URL param
  name: string,            // display name
  location?: string,       // area / sub-market (e.g. 'Dubai Marina')
  description?: string,    // free-text description
  developerName?: string,  // developer if found
  heroImage?: string,      // URL of scraped image (or null)
  amenities?: string[],    // list of amenity strings
  priceRange?: {           // if found -- approximate average
    min: number,
    max: number,
    currency: string       // default 'AED'
  },
  sources: string[],       // which of the 3 URLs contributed data
  scrapedAt: Date          // set on first insert, NEVER expires / never re-scraped
}
```

Cache policy: No expiration. Once stored, a document is never re-scraped (same as existing project documents). scrapedAt is metadata only.

### Page UI -- /communities/[slug]
The communities path already exists (src/app/[locale]/communities/). The new page must:
- Match the visual style of the existing project detail page (hero image, name, section cards).
- Display a prominent 'Community Information' badge (not a Binayah listing).
- Show all available fields: name, location, description, developer, amenities, price range.
- Omit entirely: unit tabs, floor plan tabs, price-by-bedroom tables, subscribe button, favorites button.
- Include one soft CTA banner at the bottom with copy: 'Interested in similar properties?' -- links to the inquiry form or the main search.

### What this is NOT
- Not a listing page -- no Buy / Rent / Enquire buttons on the property.
- Not saveable as a favorite.
- Not added to the projects collection.
- No DLD API (disabled per legal note in PROJECT_CONTEXT).
- No pricing tables, no floor plans, no unit details.

### Fallback UX
- If all three scrape attempts fail or return no usable data for the searched term: do not create a document; show a graceful 'No community info found' message in the search modal -- no broken page.
- If a document exists but fields like heroImage, amenities, or priceRange are absent: page still renders with the fields it has; absent sections are hidden (not shown as empty).

### External dependencies
- BrightData Web Unlocker API -- credentials in .env.shared. Add to .env.local and push to Vercel.
- MongoDB binayah_web_new_dev (shared cluster, same as all other collections).

### Acceptance bar
1. Searching for a community name with no existing project results triggers the scrape flow.
2. On second search for the same term, cached document is served (no second BrightData call).
3. Page renders with Community Information badge, available fields, no listing buttons, and the 'Interested in similar properties?' CTA.
4. Failed scrape surfaces a graceful message, no 500 error.
5. TypeScript strict-mode compiles clean (npx tsc --noEmit).

plannerAnswers.q1: default: hook into existing search modal/bar
plannerAnswers.q2: depends on what we will be able to scrape
plannerAnswers.q4: no expiration - data stays forever, like existing projects
plannerAnswers.q6: yes to CTA but copy should say "interested in similar properties" instead of the default

plan: See stages[0].spec — Stage 1 ships the full Community Info Pages feature: /api/community-info Next.js route (Mongo cache + BrightData scraper of 3 sources), search-modal integration that surfaces a community card on zero-result queries, conditional render of /communities/[slug] for community-info docs (hero, badge, available fields only, 'Interested in similar properties?' CTA, no Favorite/Subscribe/Enquire), and BrightData env-var propagation to .env.local + Vercel. Fail gracefully if all scrapes miss. Single stage — no sequential dependency justifies splitting; the API, scraper, and UI ship together so QA can exercise the full demo path.
summary: Plan: ship community-info feature in 1 stage — /api/community-info (Mongo cache + BrightData scraper of 3 sources), search-modal hook on zero-result queries, conditional /communities/[slug] render with Community Information badge and 'Interested in similar properties?' CTA, no listing affordances. Routing to design phase for a lightweight aesthetic brief on the new page surface.

loopContext.designRouteReason: New user-visible page surface (/communities/[slug] community-info variant) with non-trivial layout decisions — badge placement, section-card hierarchy, CTA tone, hide-when-empty rules. Aesthetic brief from designer is worth the ~$1-2 to set the bar before DEV writes JSX.

designBrief.direction: Editorial community guide — the same project-detail vocabulary, restrained one notch. Match `ProjectDetailClient`'s hero-image-led layout exactly, but strip every transactional affordance and let a single accent-gold 'Community Information' badge silently reframe the page as reference, not inventory.
designBrief.directionReasoning: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES says treat sibling surfaces as siblings — readers shouldn't notice they've left the project pattern. The only honest signal that this is informational is one badge and the absence of CTAs; everything else (typography, hero ratio, card system, breadcrumbs, accent color) is identical to `/project/[slug]`. This makes content the differentiator, not chrome.

benchmark.product: The existing `ProjectDetailClient` hero + section-card layout in this repo (the 70vh hero with breadcrumbs over a darkened image, max-w-7xl content, accent-bordered info cards). Externally, Bayut's Community Guide page is the closest peer — image-led, info-dense, clearly not a listing.
benchmark.whatToMatch: The hero rhythm: full-bleed image at `h-[50vh] sm:h-[65vh] lg:h-[70vh]` with `bg-gradient-to-t from-foreground via-foreground/60 to-foreground/30` overlay, breadcrumbs in `text-white/60`, then `text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-bold text-white` title, then a single muted-white description line. Below the hero: the same `bg-card rounded-2xl border border-border/50` section-card system used for project amenities/description blocks.

surfaces.name: Hero
decisions: Reuse `CommunityDetailClient`'s hero shell verbatim — same height, same gradient, same breadcrumb rail (Home › Communities › <name>).

Title: community name in `text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-bold text-white`. Below it: location (e.g. 'Dubai Marina') in `text-white/70 text-lg` with a `MapPin` icon, only if location was scraped.

'Community Information' badge sits inline ABOVE the H1 (not floating top-right), as a small uppercase pill: `text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.2em] px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full bg-accent text-accent-foreground` — same accent gold the rest of the site uses for status badges. This is the ONE signal that reframes the page; it must be the first thing a reader's eye lands on.

Hero image: scraped `heroImage` if present. If absent, use the brand-green gradient (`linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)`) full-bleed with a single centered `Building2` lucide icon at 96px in `text-white/20`. Never a stock photo, never `/assets/dubai-hero.webp` — that's the listing fallback and it lies about what this page is.
Forbidden in hero: Favorite heart, Subscribe bell, Share button, language/currency switchers tied to listings.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Principle: 'show what's true, not a symbol of it.' The badge tells the user this is reference material before they scroll. Reusing the project hero shell verbatim honors 'siblings, not strangers' — readers shouldn't have to relearn the page.

surfaces.name: Body — section cards
decisions: Single column on mobile, two-column on `lg:` (description left, sidebar with location/developer/price-range right). Container: `max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6` matching project detail.

Each section is a `bg-card rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 border border-border/50` card with a small accent-gold rule `<div className='h-[2px] w-8 rounded-full bg-gradient-to-r from-accent to-accent/60 mb-3' />` above the section heading — same eyebrow pattern used in `ProjectDetailClient` lines 571/596.
Section heading style: `text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-accent font-semibold` eyebrow + `text-2xl font-bold text-foreground` title, per the Section Label Pattern in DESIGN_SYSTEM.md.

Sections, in order, each rendered ONLY if its data exists: (1) About — description paragraph, max two columns of `text-base text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed`. (2) Location — single line with `MapPin` icon. (3) Developer — single line with `Building` icon and developer name. (4) Amenities — chip grid: `inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 rounded-lg bg-muted text-sm text-foreground` with a small lucide icon per chip (use a single neutral `CheckCircle2` if you can't reliably map icons — better than wrong icons). (5) Price range — `AED 1.5M – 3.2M` formatted with the existing `formatProjectPrice` helper, one-liner with `Wallet` icon.

Empty-section rule: if a field is missing, the entire card is omitted — no 'Not available' placeholders, no skeleton, no greyed-out shells. The page silently shrinks. (DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: 'absence is honest; placeholders lie.')
Forbidden body elements: unit type tabs, floor-plan grid, payment-plan ladder, 'Reserve a unit' CTA strip, agent contact card, brochure download, video tour block.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Reusing project-detail's section-card chassis means zero new components and DEV can copy patterns directly from `ProjectDetailClient.tsx`. Hide-when-empty enforces honesty — a Marina Gate 1 page with three cards reads as confidently as a five-card page when the cards it shows are real.

surfaces.name: Bottom CTA — 'Interested in similar properties?'
decisions: Full-width banner section (NOT a floating button), `py-16 sm:py-20`, background: brand-green gradient `linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)` — the same surface used for primary CTAs across the site.

Centered content stack: small accent eyebrow 'Looking for a home here?' (`text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-accent font-semibold`), then h2 'Interested in similar properties?' in `text-3xl sm:text-4xl font-bold text-white`, then one muted line: 'Browse Binayah's verified listings nearby.' Then ONE button.

Button: gold gradient (the secondary CTA pattern from DESIGN_SYSTEM lines 150-160) — `style={{ background: 'linear-gradient(to right, #D4A847, #B8922F)', boxShadow: '0 4px 20px rgba(212,168,71,0.3)' }}`, copy 'Browse Properties' or 'Explore Listings', linking to `/properties` (or `/?search=<community location>` if the location was scraped — DEV decides which yields better results). Single button only.
Forbidden: a second WhatsApp/Call button beside it (the floating WhatsApp FAB is already on the page — don't double up). No phone number inline. No 'Contact agent' card.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Soft tone, hard placement. Putting it at the bottom as a banner (not a sticky bar, not a popup) keeps the page informational top-to-bottom. Brand-green background distinguishes it as the page's one moment of intent — readers scroll INTO the offer, they aren't pushed by it.

designBrief.motion: Hero title: `motion.div` fade-up `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }} animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` matching the existing community page (line 52). Section cards: stagger fade-up on viewport enter, `whileInView` with `delay: i * 0.08`, same as the project list cards. CTA banner: no entrance animation — it's a destination, not a reveal. Hover on the gold button: `hover:-translate-y-0.5` per the standard lift convention. Nothing else animates.

copy.voice: Second-person warm, factual. The page is documentary, not aspirational — Wikipedia tone with Binayah's hospitality.

designBrief.colorExtensions: Uses existing palette only. Badge = `bg-accent text-accent-foreground` (gold). Hero gradient overlay = existing foreground stops. CTA banner = brand green gradient `#0B3D2E → #1A7A5A` verbatim. CTA button = gold gradient `#D4A847 → #B8922F` verbatim. No new tokens.
designBrief.layoutSketch:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  NAVBAR                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ░░ Hero image (or brand-green fallback) │
│ ░░ Home › Communities › Marina Gate 1   │
│ ░░  [COMMUNITY INFORMATION]  ← gold pill│
│ ░░  Marina Gate 1                       │
│ ░░  📍 Dubai Marina                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ───── ABOUT                            │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐      │
│  │ Description │  │ Location    │      │
│  │ paragraph   │  │ Developer   │      │
│  │             │  │ Price range │      │
│  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘      │
│  ───── AMENITIES (only if present)      │
│  [chip] [chip] [chip] [chip]            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ░░ BRAND GREEN BANNER ░░                │
│ ░░ Looking for a home here?             │
│ ░░ Interested in similar properties?    │
│ ░░ [ Browse Properties ] ← gold button  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  FOOTER                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
doNot: Do not show empty section cards with '—' or 'Not available'. Hide the entire card when its data is missing.

Do not fall back to `/assets/dubai-hero.webp` when the scraped hero image is missing — that image is the listing-page fallback and conflates this page with sellable inventory. Use the brand-green gradient with a Building2 icon instead.
Do not add a Favorite heart, Subscribe bell, Buy/Rent/Enquire buttons, agent contact card, or brochure download anywhere on the page.
Do not invent amenity icons you can't verify. If you can't map an amenity string to a confident lucide icon, render the chip with a single CheckCircle2 — wrong icons are worse than uniform ones.
Do not make the 'Community Information' badge dismissible or animate it. It's a permanent label, not a notification.
Do not use placeholder text like 'Description coming soon' — if `description` is empty, the About card is omitted entirely.
Do not add a sticky CTA bar, modal, or floating button for the 'similar properties' link. It's a banner at the bottom, in-flow.
Do not introduce any new green hex value. Only `#0B3D2E` / `#1A7A5A` / accent-gold tokens. REVIEWER will flag any deviation.
